title: PCR Reaction Optimization
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"emgq4","text":"How to run nucleic acid amplification using the Thermo scientific PCR Master Mix kit.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d7vrf","text":"Each reaction produces 50 μL.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"dkroq","text":"For the original protocol, look at:  .","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":36,"length":1}],"data":{}},{"key":"fciq2","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"al29n","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"file","mutability":"MUTABLE","data":{"guid":"E8202D2F342911EEAFD10A58A9FEAC02","original_name":"PCR Master Mix Manual.pdf","placeholder":"https://www.protocols.io/img/extensions/pdf.png","source":"https://content.protocols.io/bdewa.pdf"}}}}
guidelines: {"blocks":[{"key":"bou5h","text":"Gloves must be worn at all times.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"6in2i","text":"Use all precautions to avoid contamination when making reaction mixture.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7i0of","text":"Always pipette mix each reagent in aliquot before pipetting.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"cqdeh","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"6x4fP","text":"MATERIALS","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":null,"data":null},{"key":"ktM0t","text":" ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":null,"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":0,"length":1}],"data":null},{"key":"f7cm","text":"70% ethanol solution in DI water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"kllp","text":"RNAway","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"e69nv","text":"Thermo Scientific Master Mix (2x)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"b75a4","text":"PCR primer mix (25 μM)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"cusia","text":"Target DNA or RNA","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8ppd0","text":"RNAse free water","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"reagents","mutability":"","data":{"id":119310,"guid":"419821DC341F11EE9D4302C0B41BC903","name":"DNAse/RNAse free distilled water","mol_weight":0,"sku":"10977023","url":"","cas_number":"","rrid":"","vendor":{"id":95,"name":"Thermo Fisher Scientific","link":"https://www.thermofisher.com/us/en/home.html","is_requested":false,"image":{"source":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png","placeholder":"/img/vendors/placeholder.png"}},"is_public":true,"can_edit":false}}}}
steps: Spray entire work area with 70% EtOH including pipettes, tip holder used for holding PCR tubes, and work surface. Wipe with a paper towel.
Spray entire work area with RNAway.
Take styrofoam container to Marley 527 (directly across from Marley 509) and fill halfway with ice.
Set PCR tube holder on ice, and allow to cool for 00:03:00 .
Transfer Master Mix, primers, RNAse free water, and target tubes from freezer to PCR tube holder on ice.
Allow reagents to thaw on ice

Carefully obtain (2) 0.2 mL PCR tubes. Label one with "NTC" and the other "TARG". These will be your reaction vessels. To avoid contamination when grabbing PCR tubes, only touch the outside of tubes. Avoid touching the inside of the caps of other tubes in this process. This is critical.
Add the following to your two tubes: %s %s  Various primer concentrations are to be optimized. Start with 1.0 μL (0.5 μM).
Vortex mix all reagents for approximately 00:00:05 .
Hit PROCEED to select a reaction cycle.
Scroll using the '<' and '>' keys to get to EPCR. Begin using the following program: %s %s
Spin down all reagents for approximately 00:00:05 .
creator: Kenneth Schackart
